name: pyperclip (Cross-Platform Clipboard for Python)

performance.notes: System call overhead; effectively instant for human use

agent_readiness.notes: Cross-platform clipboard access for Python. import pyperclip. pyperclip.copy('Hello World'). text = pyperclip.paste(). Platforms: macOS (pbcopy/pbpaste), Windows (ctypes win32), Linux (xclip or xsel required). PyperclipException if no clipboard mechanism found. Headless Linux: install xclip (apt install xclip) or xdotool. Text only — no binary/image clipboard. Alternative: clipboard module. xerox is another alternative. Used heavily in CLI tools for putting output on clipboard. Simple, zero-dependency on macOS/Windows.

gotchas: Linux headless: requires xclip or xsel installed separately
Text-only — cannot copy images or binary data
WSL: clipboard integration requires additional setup
No async support — blocks while accessing clipboard
Clipboard contents may be lost when app exits on some platforms
best_when: CLI tools that put output on clipboard automatically
Desktop automation scripts needing clipboard read/write
Developer tools generating code snippets to clipboard
Simple cross-platform clipboard integration in Python scripts
avoid_when: When the user's GUI framework has built-in clipboard support
summary: pyperclip provides cross-platform clipboard for Python. import pyperclip; pyperclip.copy('text'); text = pyperclip.paste(). macOS (pbcopy/pbpaste), Windows (ctypes), Linux (xclip/xsel required). Text only. BSD-3 by Al Sweigart. Zero dependencies on macOS/Windows. Linux needs xclip installed. Best for CLI tools putting output on clipboard. Not for headless servers.
